questions.question: What is a line of symmetry?
questions.correct_answer: A line that divides a figure into two identical parts
questions.explanation: It's a line where one half of the figure can be folded onto the other half to create an identical shape

questions.question: Is a circle symmetrical?
questions.explanation: A circle is perfectly round and has rotational symmetry, meaning it looks the same when rotated by any angle

questions.question: Can you draw a triangle with exactly two lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A triangle can have zero, one, or three lines of symmetry but not exactly two

questions.question: What is reflection symmetry?
questions.correct_answer: When a figure looks the same after being reflected across a line
questions.explanation: It's like flipping a shape over a line to get an identical mirror image

questions.question: Can you create a symmetrical figure by folding paper and cutting it?
questions.explanation: You can use various techniques, such as folding in half and cutting along a line, to create symmetrical shapes

questions.question: What is rotational symmetry?
questions.correct_answer: When a figure looks the same after being rotated by a certain angle
questions.explanation: It's like spinning a shape around a fixed point to get an identical copy

questions.question: Can you have rotational symmetry without having a line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: Rotational symmetry is about looking the same after rotation, while line of symmetry is about folding to create identical halves

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry can a square have?
questions.explanation: A square has two diagonals that act as lines of symmetry

questions.question: Can you draw a figure with exactly four lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A rectangle has two horizontal and two vertical lines of symmetry, making it a symmetrical shape

questions.question: What is the fixed point about which an object is rotated in rotational symmetry?
questions.explanation: It's the point around which the figure spins to look identical again

important_notes.title: Line of Symmetry
important_notes.content: A line that divides a figure into two identical parts, allowing for folding and matching of shapes

important_notes.title: Reflection Symmetry
important_notes.content: When a figure looks the same after being reflected across a line, creating an identical mirror image

important_notes.title: Rotational Symmetry
important_notes.content: When a figure looks the same after being rotated by a certain angle around a fixed point

important_notes.title: Symmetrical Figures
important_notes.content: Can be created using various techniques, such as folding and cutting paper, to produce identical shapes

important_notes.title: Types of Symmetry
important_notes.content: Line symmetry (folding), reflection symmetry (mirror image), rotational symmetry (spinning around a point)

important_notes.title: Properties of a Square
important_notes.content: A square has two diagonals as lines of symmetry and is also a symmetrical shape with rotational symmetry

important_notes.title: Types of Shapes with Rotational Symmetry
important_notes.content: Circles, squares, rectangles, hexagons, and other regular polygons have rotational symmetry

important_notes.title: Properties of a Rectangle
important_notes.content: A rectangle has two horizontal and two vertical lines of symmetry, making it a symmetrical shape with rotational symmetry

important_notes.title: Types of Shapes with Line Symmetry
important_notes.content: Regular polygons (like triangles, squares, hexagons), rectangles, and other shapes that can be divided into identical parts by folding

important_notes.title: Importance of Symmetry in Real Life
important_notes.content: Symmetry is used extensively in art, architecture, design, and engineering to create aesthetically pleasing and functional structures

facts.fact: The word 'symmetry' comes from the Greek words 'syn' (together) and 'metron' (measure)
Symmetry is used in nature, such as in flowers, leaves, and animal bodies
The concept of symmetry has been studied by mathematicians and scientists for centuries
Symmetry plays a crucial role in understanding the laws of physics and the behavior of particles at a subatomic level
In art, symmetry is used to create balance and harmony in compositions
Symmetrical shapes have unique properties that make them useful in various fields, such as engineering and architecture
The study of symmetry has led to the development of new mathematical concepts and theories
Symmetry is used in many everyday objects, such as logos, currency symbols, and building designs
In computer graphics and animation, symmetry is used to create realistic movements and reflections
The concept of symmetry has been applied in music and dance to create harmonious and balanced compositions
